top5.title: AI Extinction Risk Urgency
top5.reach: Experts from AI labs to major tech CEOs and researchers have publicly discussed existential risk, yet the debate divides even credible scientists.
top5.the_fear: Artificial intelligence could pose an existential risk to humanity comparable to pandemics or nuclear war.
top5.why_contested: Some leading AI researchers assign 5-10% probability of human extinction from AI, while others dismiss existential risks as science fiction or claim other priorities are more pressing. Meta's Yann LeCun has criticized the concern as premature, while Geoffrey Hinton and Yoshua Bengio express grave concerns.
top5.case_for: Hundreds of AI experts signed statements saying AI extinction risk should be a global priority; expert surveys show roughly 40% of respondents assess >10% extinction probability.
top5.case_against: Skeptics argue AI won't become superintelligent soon enough to threaten humanity, point to lack of tangible evidence for extinction scenarios, and contend other threats like pandemics deserve prioritization.
questions: How rapidly will AGI achieve superhuman capabilities?
Can alignment strategies effectively constrain advanced AI systems?
Are extinction scenarios technically plausible or theoretical speculation?

top5.title: Global Inflation Persistence Risk
top5.reach: Central banks, major financial institutions, and economists worldwide actively debate inflation trajectories with fundamentally different baseline expectations for 2026.
top5.the_fear: Inflation could remain sticky above target levels, eroding purchasing power and forcing prolonged monetary tightening.
top5.why_contested: Hawks argue inflation is normalizing and could become entrenched, pointing to elevated government debt and wage pressures; doves counter that labor market weakness and tariff effects are temporary, forecasting disinflation.
top5.case_for: Federal debt exceeds economy size for first time since WWII; consumer inflation expectations may be unmooring; wage-price spirals historically difficult to break.
top5.case_against: Recent inflation mostly reflects one-time tariff shocks and energy prices; core inflation has declined; AI productivity gains and labor market slackness should support disinflation without recession.
questions: How much do tariff effects vs. structural factors drive current inflation?
Will central banks maintain independence if inflation targets are missed?
Can AI productivity gains outpace wage growth pressures?

top5.title: Climate Security Threat Prioritization
top5.reach: Intelligence agencies, governments, and scientific bodies globally debate whether climate poses imminent national security risk compared to geopolitical conflicts.
top5.the_fear: Climate change and ecosystem collapse pose catastrophic cascading security risks including conflict, migration, resource scarcity, and state failure.
top5.why_contested: UK issued biodiversity-security warning and scientific calls for global climate risk assessment, yet US Intelligence Community omitted climate from 2025-2026 threat assessments under Trump Administration, claiming other priorities dominate.
top5.case_for: Three hottest years on record; ecosystem collapse already underway; food/water security threats accelerating; biodiversity loss directly enables extremist recruitment.
top5.case_against: Immediate geopolitical conflicts (Iran, Ukraine, Taiwan tensions) demand urgent resources; climate impacts unfold over decades; public concern ranks climate 11th behind crime and inflation.
questions: Should intelligence analysis emphasize imminent security threats or long-term systemic risks?
How certain are cascading ecosystem collapse scenarios?
Can societies effectively address both acute conflicts and chronic climate risks simultaneously?

top5.title: Water Scarcity as Weapon Threat
top5.reach: UN, intelligence assessments, and hydropower nations actively debate whether water disputes risk military escalation or remain manageable through diplomacy.
top5.the_fear: Half of humanity lives under water stress with no governance framework; nuclear-armed rivals weaponizing rivers and dams could trigger regional war.
top5.why_contested: Some experts warn of immediate crisis potential with loaded weapons (Indus treaty suspended, Ethiopia dam operational without binding agreements); others argue economic interdependence and precedent for negotiated solutions prevent escalation.
top5.case_for: Strategic dams now built without downstream treaties; nuclear-armed India-Pakistan tensions rising; African extremists exploit water scarcity to recruit.
top5.case_against: Water disputes historically settled through negotiation despite tensions; economic costs of war exceed water benefits; modern desalination technology reduces scarcity pressure.
questions: Which water conflicts pose imminent war risk versus long-term diplomatic challenge?
How effective are international water treaties in preventing weaponization?
Can technology mitigate scarcity before geopolitical tensions explode?

top5.title: US Government Institutional Risk
top5.reach: Global analysts, foreign policy institutions, and international observers debate whether Trump Administration policies destabilize American governance or represent legitimate domestic preference realignment.
top5.the_fear: US political system experiencing disruption to checks and balances, potentially undermining reliability as ally and risk management capacity.
top5.why_contested: Some experts assess Trump dismantling checks on power and weaponizing government machinery as existential governance threat; others view aggressive presidential action as constitutional use of authority reflecting voter preferences.
top5.case_for: Attempted retaliation against political enemies, elimination of independent agencies, executive overreach in tariffs; allies express concern about US reliability and postwar frameworks fracturing.
top5.case_against: Presidents have broad constitutional powers; policy shifts reflect electoral outcomes; democratic processes ultimately constrain abuse; institutional resilience has proven robust.
questions: What specific institutional safeguards have been compromised versus adapted?
How do voters' policy preferences differ from threats to democratic norms?
Can global allies manage partnership uncertainty without institutional collapse?

honorable_mentions.title: AI Bubble Risk
honorable_mentions.summary: Financial markets question whether AI infrastructure investment ($400B+ annually) will generate returns, with some predicting bubble burst while others see transformative productivity gains.

honorable_mentions.title: Geoeconomic Confrontation Escalation
honorable_mentions.summary: Economic tools (tariffs, sanctions, investment restrictions) increasingly weaponized; experts split on whether this represents normal competition or systemic fragmentation risk.

honorable_mentions.title: Recession Probability 2026
honorable_mentions.summary: Forecasters range from 1.5-2.4% US growth with low recession odds to scenarios where tariff shock and policy errors trigger mild contraction—wide disagreement on baseline.

honorable_mentions.title: Migration and Demographic Transition Risk
honorable_mentions.summary: Some warn demographic shifts, climate migration, and Gen Z youth bulges in Global South create instability; others note historical resilience and economic benefits of migration.

honorable_mentions.title: Nuclear Weapons Proliferation Escalation
honorable_mentions.summary: Japan and South Korea considering nuclear capabilities amid US reliability concerns; experts debate whether this stabilizes deterrence or triggers Asian arms race.
